commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A maintainer should decide the OAuth redirect compatibility policy and require contributor live OAuth proof before merge; this is not a safe automation repair until that direction is chosen.; Needs attention: Needs attention because the PR changes OAuth redirect metadata and a less-recommended localhost default without upgrade proof; no dependency or workflow supply-chain changes were found.; Review finding:... (sha=c39385051f230d269f49521cd9269e56d0374c10)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes MCP OAuth's default redirect URL, normalizes MCP HTTP fetch responses before SDK OAuth error parsing, and repairs TypeBox-incompatible JSON Schema regex patterns with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +45, Tests +69. Total +114 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: Partially: the TypeBox regex crash is source-reproducible because TypeBox compiles schema patterns with the unicode flag, and the linked issue provides public PayPal/Calendly steps. This review did not establish a live current-main OAuth repro for the redirect and error-body paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should decide the OAuth redirect compatibility policy and require contributor live OAuth proof before merge; this is not a safe automation repair until that direction is chosen.

target.title: fix(mcp): repair OAuth redirect, errors, and unicode schema patterns
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix MCP OAuth dynamic client registration failures against authorization servers that reject `127.0.0.1` loopback redirects by defaulting to `http://localhost:8989/oauth/callback`.
- Surface real OAuth error bodies instead of `"[object Response]"` by normalizing MCP HTTP fetch responses to global `Response` objects the MCP SDK can read.
- Prevent post-auth MCP probe crashes on PayPal-like tool schemas whose JSON Schema `pattern` values use redundant `\:` escapes that are invalid under TypeBox's unicode RegExp compile path.

Why does this matter now?

Remote streamable-http MCP servers with OAuth (PayPal, Calendly) are currently unusable or fail with opaque errors after login.

What is the intended outcome?

Operators can run `openclaw mcp login` / `openclaw mcp probe` against OAuth MCP servers without regex compile crashes, with readable DCR errors, and with a localhost-compatible default redirect URI.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Live end-to-end OAuth against PayPal/Calendly in this PR environment
- New configurable redirect fallback policy beyond the existing `--oauth-redirect-url` override
- Upstream MCP SDK changes

What does success look like?

Regression tests cover all three failure modes; maintainers can confirm live OAuth on PayPal/Calendly with the reporter's repro steps.

What should reviewers focus on?

Loopback redirect default change (`localhost` vs `127.0.0.1`) and the conservative JSON Schema pattern repair applied before TypeBox compile.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91433

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #91433 (ClawSweeper review and reporter follow-up confirming localhost fixes Calendly)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — community bug report with maintainer review pending.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: MCP OAuth regex crash, `[object Response]` error masking, Calendly DCR redirect rejection
- Real environment tested: Local vitest on Windows checkout (`E:/Projects/openclaw`), branch `fix/issue-91433-mcp-oauth-regex`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-oauth.test.ts src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "redundant unicode-invalid"`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on changed files

```json
{
  "redirectDefault": "http://localhost:8989/oauth/callback",
  "patternBefore": "^https\\:\\/\\/",
  "patternCompileWithU": "SyntaxError: Invalid regular expression",
  "patternAfterRepair": "^https:\\/\\/",
  "parseErrorResponseBefore": "[object Response]",
  "parseErrorResponseAfter": "bad redirect"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: New regression tests pass; OAuth error parsing reads JSON body; TypeBox validator compiles PayPal-like URL patterns.
- What was not tested: Live `openclaw mcp login/probe` against PayPal or Calendly
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: No access to external OAuth AS credentials/endpoints in this environment; reporter and ClawSweeper already provided live Calendly confirmation for localhost redirect.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #91433 repro logs (`Invalid regular expression: /^https\:\/\//u`, `[object Response]`)

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-oauth.test.ts src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts -t "redundant unicode-invalid"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on changed source/test files

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `mcp-oauth.test.ts`: default redirect uses localhost
- `mcp-http-fetch.test.ts`: cross-realm fetch response works with MCP SDK `parseErrorResponse`
- `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`: unicode-invalid JSON Schema pattern compiles and validates

What failed before this fix, if known?

- PayPal probe: `Invalid regular expression: /^https\:\/\//u: Invalid escape`
- Calendly login: `Invalid OAuth error response ... "[object Response]"`
- Calendly DCR: `invalid_client_metadata` for `127.0.0.1` redirect (reporter confirmed localhost works)

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regression tests added for all three fixes.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Default OAuth redirect host changes from `127.0.0.1` to `localhost`; operators with explicit `--oauth-redirect-url` are unaffected.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes`)

OAuth redirect registration metadata and MCP HTTP response wrapping for OAuth error readability.

What is the highest-risk area?

Changing the default loopback redirect host for dynamic client registration.

How is that risk mitigated?

RFC 8252 permits localhost loopback; reporter verified Calendly accepts `localhost`; existing `openclaw mcp configure --oauth-redirect-url` override remains available.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer review and live OAuth proof on PayPal/Calendly per ClawSweeper acceptance criteria.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Blocked until live proof for PayPal probe path; CI and maintainer review pending after PR open.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial implementation addresses ClawSweeper-identified source paths for redirect default, error body surfacing, and TypeBox `/u` pattern compile root cause.
target.author: liuwqgit

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
